engineGuide.h2: Audi TT Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Audi TT across three generations (8N, 8J, 8S), with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The transverse EA113 2.0 TFSI defined the Mk2 TT's launch in 2006 - a belt-driven unit carrying over from the Mk5 Golf GTI with strong tuning potential.
compatibleVariants: TT 2.0 TFSI, TT 2.0 TFSI Sport, TT 2.0 TFSI S Line, TT Coupe, TT Roadster
commonFailures: Cam follower (flat tappet) wear - inspect/replace every 10-15k miles; failure destroys camshaft and HPFP
Timing belt requires replacement at ~110k miles
High pressure fuel pump failure following cam follower disintegration
entries.cta: Get quotes for BWA Audi TT engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The TTS high-output EA113 evolved through three variants from 2008-2014, climbing from 265bhp (CDLA) to 272bhp (CDLB) with a stronger bottom end and K04 turbocharger.
compatibleVariants: TTS, TTS Quattro, TTS S Line, TTS Competition, TTS Black Edition
commonFailures: Cam follower wear requires inspection every 10-15k miles
Timing belt replacement at ~110k miles
Timing chain tensioner (cam chain) can fail - no specified interval
entries.cta: Get quotes for CDLA Audi TTS engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The EA888 Gen 2 brought chain-driven camshafts and revised piston rings to the TT facelift. According to Audi, transverse variants (CESA/CETA) received updated pistons before production began.
compatibleVariants: TT 2.0 TFSI (facelift), TT 2.0 TFSI S Line, TT 2.0 TFSI Black Edition, TT Coupe, TT Roadster
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (pre-2012) - catastrophic valvetrain damage if not addressed
Chain stretch requiring full timing replacement before 150k miles
Oil consumption from piston ring wear (some reports, disputed for transverse fitment)
entries.cta: Get quotes for CESA Audi TT engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The 1.8 TFSI EA888 Gen 2 offered a lower-cost entry point to Mk2 TT ownership from 2007-2011, sharing the chain-driven architecture of its 2.0-litre stablemate.
compatibleVariants: TT 1.8 TFSI, TT 1.8 TFSI Sport, TT 1.8 TFSI S Line
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (pre-2012) - catastrophic engine damage
Chain stretch requiring replacement at 90k-150k miles
Roller tappet HPFP design is "lifetime" but replace during rebuild
entries.cta: Get quotes for CDAA Audi TT engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The narrow-angle VR6 delivered a naturally-aspirated V6 soundtrack unique to the TT range, shared with the Golf R32. Owners prize its reliability and distinctive exhaust note over forced-induction alternatives.
compatibleVariants: TT 3.2 VR6, TT 3.2 VR6 Quattro, TT 3.2 VR6 S Line
commonFailures: Timing chain and guide wear at 90k-120k miles
Oil leaks from timing cover control valve seals (common high-mileage issue)
DSG gearbox-related issues rather than engine-specific failures
entries.cta: Get quotes for BUB Audi TT engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The legendary five-cylinder EA855 transformed the TT into a genuine RS-badged icon - its unique firing order and turbocharged character making it a cult classic among performance enthusiasts.
compatibleVariants: TT RS, TT RS Plus, TT RS Quattro, TT RS Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain and guide wear requiring replacement at 120k-150k miles
Valve train failure on high-mileage or tuned examples
Roller tappet HPFP - "lifetime" design but replace during rebuild
entries.cta: Get quotes for CEPA Audi TT RS engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The 8S-generation TT RS received the evolved EA855 evo with 400bhp, aluminium block construction, and significant internal upgrades over the earlier 8J five-cylinder.
compatibleVariants: TT RS (8S), TT RS Carbon Edition, TT RS Sport
commonFailures: Oil pump failure on early DAZA units
Water pump leaks (common EA855 evo issue)
Timing chain stretch requiring replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for DAZA Audi TT RS engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The 8S TT launched with the EA888 Gen 3, bringing higher compression, integrated exhaust manifold, and improved thermal management to the third-generation sports coupe.
compatibleVariants: TT 2.0 TFSI (8S), TT 2.0 TFSI S Line, TT 2.0 TFSI Black Edition, TT Coupe, TT Roadster
commonFailures: Coolant loss from water pump/thermostat housing failure
Carbon buildup on intake valves requiring walnut blasting
PCV valve failure causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for CNTC Audi TT engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The 8S TTS utilised the high-output EA888 Gen 3 with an uprated turbocharger and strengthened internals, delivering 306bhp and quattro grip as standard.
compatibleVariants: TTS (8S), TTS Quattro, TTS S Line, TTS Competition
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leakage on high-mileage units
Water pump/coolant loss issues common to EA888 Gen 3
PCV valve failure leading to oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for CJXF Audi TTS engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: The 2.0 TDI CR (Common Rail) brought diesel efficiency to the TT range for the first time, using a belt-driven camshaft and piezo injectors for improved refinement.
compatibleVariants: TT 2.0 TDI, TT 2.0 TDI Quattro, TT 2.0 TDI S Line
commonFailures: Oil pump hex drive failure (2004-2015 engines affected) - catastrophic oil pressure loss
Injector failure on high-mileage CR units
DPF blockage from short journeys
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBBB Audi TT engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: The later CFGB 2.0 TDI CR updated the common-rail architecture with modest internal revisions and remained available until the TT diesel was discontinued.
compatibleVariants: TT 2.0 TDI (facelift), TT 2.0 TDI Quattro, TT 2.0 TDI S Line, TT 2.0 TDI Ultra
commonFailures: Oil pump hex drive remains vulnerable despite revisions
Cambelt replacement required every ~110k miles
DPF and EGR system clogging on short-trip cars
entries.cta: Get quotes for CFGB Audi TT engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The high-output 1.8T 20v BAM engine powered the TT 225 Quattro Sport, becoming an icon of early 2000s tuning culture with its robust bottom end and K04 turbocharger.
compatibleVariants: TT 1.8T Quattro Sport, TT 225, TT 240 Quattro Sport (BFV variant)
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals - catastrophic bearing failure
Ignition coil pack failure (common across all 1.8T)
Turbocharger oil seal leak on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for BAM Audi TT engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The 180bhp 1.8T AJQ powered entry-level Mk1 TT models with a K03 turbocharger, sharing the 20v architecture of its more powerful BAM sibling.
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup leading to turbo failure
Coil pack failure
Camshaft position sensor failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJQ Audi TT engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Audi TT Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: EA888 Gen 2 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: EA888 Gen 2 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - The Most Common TT Engine Catastrophe
problems.affectedModels: Audi TT 1.8 TFSI (2007-2011, CDAA), TT 2.0 TFSI facelift (2009-2012, CESA, CETA), TT S Line, TT Black Edition
problems.rootCause: The original chain tensioner uses a weak ratchet mechanism that fails to maintain oil pressure on startup, allowing the timing chain to skip teeth and pistons to collide with open valves.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner with revised 2012+ part, timing chain, guides, and sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix with revised components - prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt EA888 Gen 2 with upgraded tensioner, chains, and pistons
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - engine is better than factory

problems.recommendation: If your TT's EA888 engine rattles on cold start for more than one second, replace the tensioner and chain immediately as a preventative measure - it costs £1,200-£1,800 at a specialist and saves a £4,000+ replacement. On a TT worth £4,000-£7,000, once the chain has jumped, fit a rebuilt unit. Repairing bent valves on the original engine often exceeds replacement cost.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EA888 engine prices for your Audi TT

problems.h4: EA113 Cam Follower (Flat Tappet) Failure - The Hidden Engine Killer
problems.affectedModels: Audi TT 2.0 TFSI (2006-2009, BWA), TT 2.0 TFSI Sport, TT 2.0 TFSI S Line
problems.rootCause: The fuel pump cam follower is a consumable wear item that Audi did not list as a serviceable component. When it wears through, the camshaft lobe grinds directly against the high-pressure fuel pump, destroying both.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cam follower only - inspect camshaft and HPFP
Replace inlet camshaft, high-pressure fuel pump, and all followers
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix but expensive after failure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine (extensive internal damage)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EA113 with revised camshaft and HPFP
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty - for engines with bearing damage

problems.recommendation: Replace the cam follower on your BWA-engined TT every 30,000-40,000 miles. The part costs £30-£50 and an hour of labour. This single service item prevents a £1,000+ repair or a £2,500+ engine replacement. If your TT has already suffered camshaft damage, compare specialist prices - dealer quotes for this repair are often close to rebuilt engine costs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BWA EA113 engine replacement and cam follower repairs

problems.h4: VR6 Timing Chain & Guide Wear - The 3.2 Litre's Expensive Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Audi TT 3.2 VR6 (2003-2010, BUB), TT 3.2 VR6 Quattro, TT 3.2 VR6 S Line
problems.rootCause: The VR6's dual overhead camshafts are driven by chains running through plastic guides. These guides become brittle with age and heat cycles, eventually shattering and allowing chains to jump timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chains, tensioners, guides, and camshaft adjusters
Supply & fit rebuilt 3.2 VR6 with updated chain components and guides
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty - for engines with bent valves

problems.recommendation: On a TT 3.2 VR6 worth £5,000-£8,000, preventative chain and guide replacement is the smartest long-term decision - specialist pricing around £1,600-£2,400 is significantly less than a rebuilt engine. Listen for chain rattle on cold start, especially between 90k-120k miles. Once the engine has jumped timing, replacement is usually better value than repairing the original head.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt BUB VR6 engine prices for your Audi TT

problems.group: EA113 High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Camshaft Failure
problems.h4: EA113 High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Camshaft Failure
problems.affectedModels: Audi TT 2.0 TFSI (2006-2009, BWA), Audi TTS (2008-2014, CDLA, CDMA)
problems.rootCause: When the cam follower wears through completely, the HPFP plunger contacts the camshaft lobe directly. Within minutes of driving, the lobe is ground flat, destroying the camshaft and sending metal particles through the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace inlet camshaft, high-pressure pump, cam follower, and oil change
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix - requires immediate attention at first symptom

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine (contaminated oil system)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EA113 with new camshaft, HPFP, oil pump, and bearings
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty - metal debris often damages bottom end

problems.recommendation: If your BWA-engined TT has suffered cam follower failure, stop driving immediately. If caught early with no metal debris circulated, camshaft and HPFP replacement at a specialist (£1,000-£1,600) saves the engine. However, if you've driven more than 50 miles after the failure, the oil system is likely contaminated - we recommend a rebuilt engine for long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare EA113 engine replacement quotes for your TT

problems.group: 2.0 TDI Oil Pump Hex Drive Failure
problems.h4: 2.0 TDI Oil Pump Hex Drive Failure - Catastrophic Oil Pressure Loss
problems.affectedModels: Audi TT 2.0 TDI (2008-2014, CBBB, CFGB), TT 2.0 TDI Quattro
problems.rootCause: The oil pump is driven by a hex key pressed into the balancer shaft. Over time, the hex key rounds off, the oil pump stops turning, and oil pressure drops to zero without warning, destroying the bottom end.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - Oil Pump Hex Drive Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hex drive with upgraded steel key and oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix - removes known failure point

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2.0 TDI with upgraded oil pump drive and bearings

problems.recommendation: On a TT 2.0 TDI, proactive hex drive replacement at 80k-100k miles costs £700-£1,100 at a specialist. Once the hex drive fails, the engine is almost always scrap - catastrophic oil pressure loss damages crank journals and bearings beyond economical repair. If you've lost oil pressure, fit a rebuilt unit rather than attempting to salvage the original bottom end.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.0 TDI engine prices for your Audi TT

problems.group: EA888 Gen 3 Water Pump & Thermostat Housing Failure
problems.h4: EA888 Gen 3 Water Pump & Thermostat Housing Failure - Coolant Loss
problems.affectedModels: Audi TT 2.0 TFSI (2014-2023, CNTC), Audi TTS (2014-2023, CJXF)
problems.rootCause: The plastic water pump and thermostat housing assembly develops hairline cracks around the mounting flange or internal seals fail, causing slow coolant loss without visible external leaks in many cases.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Water Pump & Thermostat Housing Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat housing, and coolant
Replace cylinder head gasket, check for warpage, water pump assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Uncommon if coolant level is maintained

problems.recommendation: On 8S-generation TT petrol models, monitor coolant levels monthly. The water pump is a known weak point but replacement is affordable at a specialist (£400-£650). Do not ignore low coolant warnings - an overheated EA888 Gen 3 often warps the cylinder head, turning a £650 repair into a £2,000+ one. This failure rarely requires full engine replacement unless completely neglected.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CNTC and CJXF engine repairs and replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Audi TT
finalCta.paragraph: The Audi TT's engine range has documented weak points, but most are manageable with preventative maintenance or a specialist-fitted rebuilt unit. Whether you own a BWA-engined Mk2 with cam follower concerns, an EA888 facelift TT with chain tensioner anxiety, or a VR6 reaching timing guide mileage, EnginesMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Audi TT specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, and nationwide delivery or fitment options. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Audi TT Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Audi TT and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Audi TT Reconditioned Engines (Recon)

types.frontDisclaimer: Not available for all engine codes - check with suppliers.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned engines are stripped, inspected and rebuilt to factory tolerances with new piston rings, bearings and seals.
backBullets: Ideal for EA888 Gen 2 (CESA, CETA) and EA113 (BWA) TT engines
12-24 month warranty - better than basic used units
Typically 10-15 working day lead time for completion

types.title: Audi TT Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive overhaul with new gaskets, timing components, pistons and bearings. Fully machined block.
types.frontDescription: Comprehensive overhaul with new gaskets, timing components, pistons and bearings. Fully machined block.
types.frontDisclaimer: Highest quality option - includes upgraded failure parts.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt engines receive new timing chains, tensioners, water pumps, piston rings and bearings throughout the assembly.
backBullets: Upgraded timing chain tensioner fitted to EA888 units
Ideal for high-mileage TTS (CDLA) and 2.0 TDI (CBBB) owners
Bench tested with documented quality control report

engineSizes.h2: Audi TT Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Audi TT 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0 TDI common-rail engine joined the TT range with the 8J generation in 2008, offering quattro or front-wheel drive configurations and genuine 50+ mpg economy. This EA288-family unit remained available until the diesel was dropped from the line-up in 2019, making it the sole diesel displacement ever fitted to the TT.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump hex drive failure leading to catastrophic oil pressure loss; injector failure on high-mileage CR units
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi TT 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Audi TT 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4 TFSI arrived with the third-generation 8S TT as the entry-level petrol option, replacing the earlier 1.8 TFSI. This EA211-series unit offered surprising refinement and efficiency for a sports coupe, though it remained a niche choice compared to the 2.0-litre alternatives.
commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger wastegate rattle; coolant loss from water pump assembly
items.cta: Compare Audi TT 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi TT 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8-litre displacement appeared across two distinct eras of TT production. The 8N-generation 1.8T 20v (EA113) defined the Mk1 TT experience with its charismatic five-valve cylinder head and turbocharged response. The 8J-generation 1.8 TFSI (EA888 Gen 2) offered a lighter, chain-driven alternative for entry-level Mk2 buyers.
commonFailurePoints: Oil sludge buildup (EA113); timing chain tensioner failure (EA888 Gen 2)
items.cta: Compare Audi TT 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi TT 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0 TFSI is the backbone of the TT petrol range, spanning all three generations with multiple families. EA113 belt-driven units (BWA) powered early 8J models, while EA888 Gen 2 (CESA/CETA) brought chain drive to facelift 8J cars. The 8S generation introduced EA888 Gen 3 (CNTC/CJXF) with outputs from 194bhp to 306bhp in TTS form.
commonFailurePoints: Cam follower wear (EA113 - BWA); timing chain tensioner failure (EA888 Gen 2 pre-2012); water pump coolant loss (EA888 Gen 3)
items.cta: Compare Audi TT 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi TT 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary 2.5-litre five-cylinder EA855 turned the TT into a genuine RS icon. Fitted exclusively to TT RS models across both 8J and 8S generations, this engine's unique firing order and turbocharged character made it a cult classic. The 8J unit produced 335-355bhp, while the 8S DAZA variant delivered 394-400bhp.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain guide wear; oil pump failure (early DAZA units); water pump leaks
items.cta: Compare Audi TT 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi TT 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.2-litre VR6 (EA390) offered a naturally aspirated alternative in the TT range, sharing its narrow-angle V6 architecture with the contemporary Golf R32. Fitted to both 8N and early 8J generations, the BUB engine delivered a distinctive V6 soundtrack and linear power delivery that enthusiasts still prize over forced-induction alternatives. Not sure which engine size your Audi TT needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EnginesMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Audi TT specialists.
commonFailurePoints: guide wear at 90k-120k miles; oil leaks from timing cover control valve seals
items.cta: Compare Audi TT 3.2L petrol engine prices

Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EnginesMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Audi TT specialists.

items.title: Audi TT Diesel Engines

items.descriptor: The 2.0 TDI was offered only in the 8J generation (2008-2014), making diesel TTs rare but highly sought-after by high-mileage drivers seeking quattro grip with 50+ mpg economy.

CFGB - facelift 2.0 TDI (2011-2014), updated ECU calibration

items.title: Audi TT Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol engines dominate the TT range across all three generations (8N, 8J, 8S) - from the tuner-favourite 1.8T 20v and belt-driven EA113 2.0 TFSI to the chain-driven EA888 Gen 2/Gen 3 and the legendary five-cylinder EA855.
items.descriptor: Petrol engines dominate the TT range across all three generations (8N, 8J, 8S) - from the tuner-favourite 1.8T 20v and belt-driven EA113 2.0 TFSI to the chain-driven EA888 Gen 2/Gen 3 and the legendary five-cylinder EA855.
items.familiesLabel: Common Audi TT Petrol Engine Families
families: AJQ/AUQ/BAM - 1.8T 20v EA113 (8N, 178-221bhp), belt drive, legendary tuner platform
BWA - 2.0 TFSI EA113 (8J, 197bhp), belt drive, cam follower critical
CESA/CETA - 2.0 TFSI EA888 Gen 2 (8J facelift, 208-211bhp), chain drive, tensioner risk
CDLA/CDMA/CDLB - TTS EA113 (8J, 265-272bhp), belt drive, K04 turbo
CNTC - 2.0 TFSI EA888 Gen 3 (8S, 194-227bhp), chain drive, water pump issues
CJXF - TTS EA888 Gen 3 (8S, 296-306bhp), uprated turbo
CEPA/CEPB - 2.5 TFSI EA855 (8J TT RS, 335-355bhp), five-cylinder cult icon
CZGB/DAZA - 2.5 TFSI EA855 evo (8S TT RS, 394-400bhp), aluminium block
BUB - 3.2 VR6 EA390 (8N/8J, 247-250bhp), narrow-angle V6, timing chain guides
foundIn: 8J TT (2006-2014) - 2.0 TFSI EA113 (BWA), TTS EA113 (CDLA/CDMA/CDLB), 1.8 TFSI EA888 (CDAA), 3.2 VR6 (BUB)
knownFor: EA113 belt drive (BWA) - cam follower wear destroys camshafts if ignored
EA888 Gen 2 (CESA/CETA) - timing chain tensioner failure pre-2012
EA888 Gen 3 (CNTC/CJXF) - water pump coolant loss, reliable otherwise
EA855 five-cylinder - cult following, unique firing order, high replacement cost
VR6 (BUB) - naturally aspirated V6 soundtrack, chain guide wear at 90k-120k

items.title: Audi TT Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: No hybrid or electrified powertrains were ever fitted to the Audi TT across any generation (8N, 8J, or 8S). The TT remained exclusively petrol or diesel throughout its 25-year production run.
items.descriptor: No hybrid or electrified powertrains were ever fitted to the Audi TT across any generation (8N, 8J, or 8S). The TT remained exclusively petrol or diesel throughout its 25-year production run.

engineYears.h2: Audi TT Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: The Audi TT was produced across three generations (8N 1998-2006, 8J 2006-2014, 8S 2014-2023) - this section maps every engine code and major change year to help you identify your unit and compare replacement prices from UK specialists.

keyChanges: Launch of Audi TT Coupe (1998) followed by Roadster (1999)
1.8T 20v EA113 series - 150bhp FWD, 180bhp Quattro, 225bhp Quattro
2003 - 3.2 VR6 (BUB) added to range
2004 - 240bhp Quattro Sport (BFV) limited edition
mainEngines: AJQ / ARY - 1.8L petrol (150bhp FWD, early cars)
knownFor: Iconic Bauhaus design - first modern Audi with retro-futuristic styling
1.8T BAM engine is a tuning legend - 300bhp achievable with bolt-ons
Timing belt crucial - failure at 60k-80k miles destroys the engine
Oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals kills turbos and bottom ends

years.ticker: 1.8T BAM engine replacement cost • 3.2 VR6 timing chain rattle • Mk1 TT Quattro Sport engine rebuild

2.0 TFSI EA113 (197bhp) replaces the 1.8T
3.2 VR6 carried over from Mk1
First TT with DSG (S-Tronic) option
knownFor: BWA engine is robust but cam follower is a critical service item (every 10k-15k miles)
3.2 VR6 offers naturally aspirated V6 character - timing chain guides wear
Early 8J cars have the simplest electronics - fewer complex modules
2.0 TFSI returns 30-35mpg driven sensibly

years.ticker: BWA cam follower failure • VR6 timing chain rattle 8J • 2.0 TFSI EA113 replacement engine

2.0 TDI CR (CBBB, 168bhp) joins the range - belt drive
1.8 TFSI EA888 (CDAA, 158bhp) added for 2009
Quattro now optional on 2.0 TFSI petrol

CDAA - 1.8L petrol EA888 Gen 1 (158bhp) - chain drive (pre-tensioner issues)
knownFor: TTS is the sweet spot - K04 turbo, stronger bottom end, same cam follower vigilance needed
2.0 TDI offers genuine 50mpg - oil pump hex drive failure is the achilles heel
CDAA 1.8 TFSI is chain-driven - early tensioners fail (pre-2012 design)
TDI rare in TT - low replacement volume but high demand from diesel enthusiasts

1.8 TFSI CDAA continues with EA888 Gen 1 architecture
TT RS (CEPA/CEPB, 335-355bhp) launched with legendary five-cylinder EA855
2010 - TTS gains CDLB (272bhp) with minor revisions
2012 - Tensioner design finally revised (post-failure)

CDLB - 2.0L petrol EA113 TTS (272bhp) - belt drive
knownFor: EA888 Gen 2 tensioner failure (pre-2012) - catastrophic when chain jumps timing
TT RS five-cylinder is a modern classic - unique firing order, strong tuning potential
2012-on tensioner (revised part) is permanent fix - check if already done
European EA888 engines (CESA/CETA) less problematic than US-spec CCTA/CBFA

2.0 TDI updated to CFGB (same 168bhp, minor revisions)

TTS unchanged (CDLB, 272bhp)
Final year for 8J generation - replaced by 8S in 2014

faq.h2: Audi TT Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What causes the timing chain rattle on startup in my Audi TT 2.0 TFSI?
items.answer: The rattle is a failing timing chain tensioner on EA888 Gen 2 engines (2009-2012). It loses oil pressure and slackens the chain. If ignored, the chain can jump and destroy your engine.
keyPoints: Rattle lasting 0.5-1 second on cold start is the classic warning symptom
Affects CESA, CETA, and CDAA engines primarily pre-2012 production
The revised tensioner (4th revision, mid-2012) is a permanent fix
Replacement costs £1,200-£1,800 at a specialist before failure occurs
Post-failure engine replacement jumps to £2,500-£4,000
items.cta: Compare EA888 timing chain repair quotes for your Audi TT

items.question: How much does an Audi TT engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Expect £1,800-£4,500 for a standard 2.0 TFSI replacement. TTS units run £2,800-£4,500, while TT RS five-cylinder engines cost £4,000-£8,000 supply only.
keyPoints: Used engine (8J 2.0 TFSI): £900-£2,200 plus £1,000-£2,000 fitting
Rebuilt engine: £2,000-£4,000 plus fitting, includes 12-month warranty
TTS (CDLA/CDLB): £2,800-£4,500 for rebuilt unit
TT RS (CEPA/DAZA): £4,000-£8,000 - specialist rebuilders only
Hidden costs: gaskets, coolant, oil, timing kit add £300-£600
items.cta: Get a tailored Audi TT engine replacement quote today

items.question: Is the 2.0 TDI engine in the TT reliable or one to avoid?
items.answer: Reliable for high-mileage drivers, but the 2.0 TDI has a well-documented Achilles heel - oil pump hex drive failure that causes sudden oil pressure loss and bottom end destruction.
keyPoints: Affects CBBB and CFGB engines (2008-2014 8J TT)
Hex drive failure typically occurs between 80,000-150,000 miles
Preventative hex drive replacement costs £700-£1,100 at a specialist
Post-failure rebuilt engine costs £2,200-£3,500
TDI offers genuine 50mpg - ideal for daily drivers
items.cta: Compare 2.0 TDI engine replacement prices for your TT

items.question: What is the cam follower issue on the BWA Audi TT engine?
items.answer: The BWA 2.0 TFSI (EA113) has a flat tappet cam follower that wears through every 30k-50k miles. When it fails, the camshaft grinds directly against the fuel pump, destroying both.
keyPoints: Fits 2006-2009 TT 2.0 TFSI (BWA, 197bhp) only - not EA888 engines
Cam follower is a £30-£50 consumable part
Replacement every 30k-40k miles costs £120-£180 at a specialist
Ignored failure destroys camshaft (£900-£1,400 repair)
Catastrophic neglect sends metal debris through the engine
items.cta: Get BWA cam follower replacement quotes for your TT

items.question: Which Audi TT engine is the most reliable to buy?
items.answer: Post-2012 EA888 Gen 2 (revised tensioner) and EA888 Gen 3 (8S) are the most reliable petrol options. The 3.2 VR6 is robust if timing guides are maintained. Avoid pre-2012 EA888 tensioner cars.
keyPoints: 2013-2014 facelift 8J (CESA/CETA with revised tensioner) - safest Mk2
8S EA888 Gen 3 (CNTC, 2014-2023) - water pump only weakness
3.2 VR6 (BUB) - bulletproof if chains and guides done at 90k-120k miles
BWA (2006-2009) - reliable if cam follower changed religiously
Pre-2012 EA888 - avoid unless tensioner already upgraded
items.cta: Compare prices for the most reliable Audi TT engines

items.question: How do I tell if my TT has an EA113 belt or EA888 chain engine?
items.answer: EA113 engines (2006-2009, BWA code) use a timing belt that needs replacement every 110k miles. EA888 engines (2009-2014, CESA/CETA/CDAA) use a chain that should last the engine's life but has tensioner issues.
keyPoints: Check engine code on VIN sticker in boot or service book
BWA, CDLA (TTS) = EA113 belt - inspect cam follower every 10k-15k miles
CESA, CETA, CDAA = EA888 chain - listen for cold start rattle
EA113 belt replacement costs £800-£1,000 at specialist
EA888 chain tensioner replacement costs £1,200-£1,800
items.cta: Get your Audi TT engine identified - enter your reg now

items.question: What are the common failure points on the TTS engine (CDLA/CDLB)?
items.answer: TTS uses the same EA113 belt engine as standard 2.0 TFSI - cam follower wear is still critical. The K04 turbo is robust but oil strainer blockage can starve the turbo on higher-mileage cars.
keyPoints: Cam follower inspection/replacement every 10k-15k miles - non-negotiable
Timing belt and water pump replacement every 110k miles
Oil pickup strainer can clog on cars with extended service intervals
K04 turbo is reliable but oil starvation kills it (£1,500+ replacement)
CDLB (272bhp) has same maintenance needs as earlier CDLA
items.cta: Compare TTS engine replacement and repair quotes

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Audi TT?
items.answer: On an 8J TT worth £3,000-£5,000, a £1,800-£3,500 rebuilt engine often makes sense if the body and interior are clean. On an 8N worth under £2,000, a used engine is the economic choice.
keyPoints: 8J TT (2006-2014) - rebuilt engine justified for cherished cars
8N TT (1998-2006) - used engine (£900-£1,800) better value
TT RS and TTS - always worth replacing due to rising values
Factor in clutch, timing kit, and servicing while engine is out
Specialist replacement maintains resale value better than DIY
items.cta: Calculate if TT engine replacement is right for your car

items.question: What does the VR6 timing chain rattle sound like on a TT 3.2?
items.answer: VR6 (BUB) timing chain rattle on cold start that lasts 1-2 seconds - caused by plastic chain guides becoming brittle. Unlike EA888, this is a gradual wear issue, not a sudden tensioner failure.
keyPoints: Affects 8N and early 8J TT 3.2 VR6 (2003-2010)
Timing chain and guide replacement costs £1,600-£2,400 at specialist
Failure typically occurs between 90,000-130,000 miles
Ignored failure leads to chain skipping timing - bent valves
Permanent fix with updated guides lasts 100k+ miles
items.cta: Get VR6 timing chain replacement quotes for your TT

items.question: Does the TT RS five-cylinder engine have any common problems?
items.answer: The EA855 five-cylinder is famously robust, but early CEPA/CEPB (8J) can suffer timing chain guide wear. Later DAZA (8S) has occasional oil pump failures. Both are rare compared to 2.0 TFSI issues.
keyPoints: 8J CEPA/CEPB (335-355bhp) - timing chain guide wear at 120k-150k miles
8S DAZA/CZGB (400bhp) - oil pump failures on early cars
Water pump leaks affect both generations
Valve train is generally bulletproof - tuning potential is huge
Replacement cost is high (£4,000-£8,000) but engines are long-lived
items.cta: Compare TT RS engine specialist prices and availability
